description_long: This is a weathered stone obelisk, likely serving as a grave marker or memorial, set atop a rectangular plinth and mounted on a larger, roughly square stone base. The obelisk is made of gray granite, with visible signs of aging and some surface discoloration. Two sides of the shaft feature engraved crosses, while the front face displays faint, partially eroded inscriptions. The base is cracked and uneven, with patches of green vegetation growing around its edges and in the crevices. The tip of the obelisk is sharply angled, giving it a distinctive, chiseled appearance. The overall structure is solid and heavy, typical of monuments found in cemeteries or parks.
description: A weathered gray granite obelisk with engraved crosses and faint inscriptions, mounted on a cracked stone base with surrounding vegetation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the inscribed face of the obelisk, with faint text and a decorative arch motif above the inscription.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a prominent engraved cross and the angled tip of the obelisk.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a plain, unadorned stone surface and visible vegetation at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another engraved cross and more visible greenery around the base.
attribute_inscription: Faint, partially eroded inscription on the front face, likely in English, with some text illegible due to weathering.
attribute_ornamentation: Engraved crosses on two opposing sides and a decorative arch motif above the inscription on the front face.
attribute_base_design: The base is a large, roughly square stone slab with visible cracks and uneven edges, supporting a smaller rectangular plinth beneath the obelisk shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply angled, chiseled to a point, with no additional decoration or embellishment.
attribute_surrounding_features: Vegetation, including small green plants and moss, growing around the base and in cracks of the stone platform.

license_info.creator_display_name: Santa Cruz Museum of Art and History
